title: Money
description: Monetary amount with currency. Used for all financial values across the data model — prices, commitments, NAV, distributions, interest payments, etc.

Money.description: A monetary value with currency. Amount is a decimal string to avoid IEEE 754 floating-point precision loss (e.g., 0.1 + 0.2 ≠ 0.3 in float). Consumers should parse amount into a fixed-precision decimal type (Python Decimal, Java BigDecimal, etc.). Example: {"amount": "50000000.00", "currency": "USD"}

amount.description: The monetary value as a decimal string. Use negative values for debits, reductions, or losses. No thousands separators. Decimal point is optional for whole amounts. Examples: '50000000.00', '-1250000.50', '0.01', '1000000'

currency.description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code. Must match the currency of the fund, facility, or deal this amount belongs to. Example: 'USD'
